nodes.label: Chord, Sustain & Percussive Rendering
nodes.rationale: DRAFT extending the single-note encoder (docs/07) to chords, sustain, strums, percussive hits on the 12-ch as-built rig. Governing principle: render the actual per-string onset + amplitude envelope detected, never a chord symbol, so the many ways to play a chord are handled for free as an honest signal-to-skin transform.

nodes.label: Chord onset encodes how it was struck (strum sweep / pluck bloom / arpeggio)
nodes.rationale: Strum = a spread sequential sweep across the string-column (down-strum low-E to high-E, up reversed, faster sweep = harder); simultaneous fingerpick/block chord = a tight bloom within ~15 ms; arpeggio is already sequential. A wrong note lands as a wrong spot in the sweep.

nodes.label: Sustain = envelope-modulated looped shimmer (LOCKED)
nodes.rationale: A held/ringing chord is re-triggered shimmer not a static hold: cycle through ringing strings at a fast stagger so each is re-triggered before it perceptually fades, the whole shimmer's amplitude follows the chord's real decay envelope, stopping when energy decays below threshold. Re-trigger because (1) adaptation desensitizes sustained fixed-site vibration (~10 dB fade), (2) simultaneous neighbors mask each other, (3) apparent motion / Tactile Brush (Israr & Poupyrev) makes discrete taps read as continuous ringing.

nodes.label: Fret-zones carry chord SHAPE (pulsed in sync) + root emphasis
nodes.rationale: Fret-zones carry the chord shape (which frets) on the torso, pulsed in sync with the shimmer (not held, so they don't add steady channels or mask); the lowest/root string gets slightly stronger / more frequent re-trigger so the chord has a felt foundation.

nodes.label: Percussive & muted hits (sharp pop across motors; muted strings don't fire)
nodes.rationale: A percussive chunk / palm-muted strum / string slap is a quick simultaneous pop across all motors (a single sharp ~15 ms transient); dead/muted strings simply don't fire their string-speaker so the felt gap is itself information (you muted the G).

nodes.label: Renderer input: per-string (onset, pitch->string/fret, envelope, decay) stream
nodes.rationale: Per active string a stream of (onset time, pitch->string/fret, amplitude envelope, offset/decay) from the engine (mic transcription + vision) for live play or read from the reference (tab/MIDI) for the target; the renderer turns it into onset-type (sweep vs bloom) -> looped shimmer (envelope-modulated) -> fret-zone shape pulses -> stop at decay threshold.
